content_en: To proceed: We have narrated, through many chains and various narrations, from Ali ibn Abu Taalib, Abdullah ibn Masood, Muaadh ibn Jabal, Abu al-Darda, ibn Umar, ibn Abbaas, Anas ibn Maalik, Abu Huraira and Abu Saeed al­ Khudrimay Allah be pleased with all of themthat the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said, "Whoever preserves for my Nation forty hadith related to its religion, Allah will then resurrect him in the company of the savants and scholars." Another narration states, "Allah will raise him as an erudite and learned scholar." In the narration from Abu al-Darda, it is stated, "On the Day of Resurrection, I will be an intercessor and witness for him." In the narration from ibn Masood, it is stated that the Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "It will be said to him, 'Enter Paradise through any door you wish."' In the narration of ibn Umar, one finds the words, "He will be recorded among the company of the scholars and will be resurrected in the company of the martyrs." The scholars of hadith agree that, although this hadith has numerous chains, it is weak. The scholars (may Allah be pleased with them) have compiled innumerable works of this nature. The first one that I know of who compiled such a work was Abdullah ibn al-Mubaarak. After him came ibn Aslam al52 Commentary on Al-Nawawi's Introduction Toosi, a pious scholar. Then came al-Hasan ibn Sufyaan al­ Nasaai, Abu Bakr al-Aajuri, Abu Bakr Muhammad ibn lbraaheem al-Asfahaani, al-Daaraqutni, al-Haakim, Abu Nuaim, Abu Abd al-Rahmaan al-Sulami, Abu Said al­ Maleeni, Abu Uthmaan al-Saabooni, Abdullah ibn Muhammad al-Ansaari, Abu Bakr al-Baihaqi and countless others both from earlier and later times. I have turned to Allah for guidance and prayed to Him while compiling these forty hadith, following the example of those Imams and guardians of Islam. The scholars have agreed that it is permissible to act in accord with weak hadith that state the virtuousness of deeds. Nonetheless, given that fact, I have not relied upon that hadith only in compiling this work. Instead, I am following the statement of the Prophet (peace be upon him) found in an authentic hadith, "Let him who was present among you inform those who are absent." The Prophet (peace be upon him) also said, "May Allah make radiant the man who has heard what I said, preserved it in his memory and conveyed it in the way that he heard it." Scholars have compiled forty hadith on faith and belief (usool), on practical matters Ø T j ణ :;.:.t :; -s4ਞ1 / ,,.. / ; "Verily, you (0 Muhammad) guide not whom you like, but Allah guides whom He wills. And He knows best those who are the guided" (al-Qasas 56). The second verse is, "And verily, you (0 Muhammad) are indeed guiding (mankind) to the Straight Path" (al-Shoora 52). Al-Nawawi stated, "I praise Him for all of His bounties. I ask Him to increase His grace and generosity." This is a general characteristic that one finds in the beginning of almost all of al-Nawawi's works. Al-Nawawi lived a very austere life. However, he fully recognized that the bounties of Allah consists of faith, knowledge and contentment. For these bounties, he regularly thanked Allah. Al-Nawawi stated, "I bear witness that our leader Muhammad is His servant and messenger, His beloved and dear one." In this passage, al­ Nawawi is specifically pointing out four important qualities of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). He was the servant of Allah. This is the most honored position that anyone could ever attain. Indeed, in the Quran, when Allah truly acclaims the position of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him), Allah refers to him as His servant. For example, at the beginning of surah al-Israa, Allah states, Commentary on Al-Nawawi's Introduction "Exalted above any imperfection be He who took His servant for a journey by night from the Masjid al-Haraam [in Makkah] to the Farthest Mosque [in Jerusalem], the neighborhood whereof we have blessed, in order that We might show him of Our signs. Verily, He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer" (al-Israa 1). This is how Allah refers to the Prophet (peace be upon him) during one of the greatest events of the Prophet's life. Al-Nawawi then points out that the Prophet (peace be upon him) was a messenger from Allah. He was chosen by Allah to communicate Allah's final message to mankind. He is the example for all of mankind to follow. However, he stands out among the messengers as being the final messenger sent for all of mankind until the Day of Judgment. When one wants to describe the Prophet (peace be upon him), it is these two terms that one should use, the servant and messenger of Allah. This is giving the Prophet (peace be upon him) his proper respect and honoring without going beyond the proper limitsas many people do today, in particular some Sufis.1 Al-Bukhari recorded that the Prophet (peace be upon him) said, J IP J. content_id:
Kemudian al-Nawawi melanjutkan: 'Kami telah meriwayatkan melalui banyak sanad dan berbagai jalur, dari Ali ibn Abi Thalib, Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Mu'adz ibn Jabal, Abu al-Darda', Ibn Umar, Ibn Abbas, Anas ibn Malik, Abu Hurairah, dan Abu Sa'id al-Khudri — semoga Allah meridai mereka semua — bahwa Rasulullah bersabda: 'Barangsiapa yang melestarikan untuk umatku empat puluh hadis yang berkaitan dengan agamanya, Allah akan membangkitkannya bersama para ulama dan cendekiawan.' Riwayat lain menyebutkan: 'Allah akan membangkitkannya sebagai seorang ulama yang alim dan terpelajar.' Dalam riwayat Abu al-Darda': 'Pada Hari Kebangkitan, aku akan menjadi pemberi syafaat dan saksi baginya.' Dalam riwayat Ibn Mas'ud: 'Akan dikatakan kepadanya: Masuklah surga dari pintu mana pun yang kamu kehendaki.' Dalam riwayat Ibn Umar: 'Ia akan dicatat dalam golongan ulama dan dibangkitkan bersama para syuhada.' Para ulama hadis sepakat bahwa meskipun hadis ini memiliki banyak sanad, hadis ini dhaif.

Para ulama — semoga Allah meridai mereka — telah menyusun karya-karya tak terhitung dalam bentuk ini. Orang pertama yang aku ketahui menyusun karya seperti ini adalah Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak. Setelah beliau muncul Ibn Aslam al-Thusi, seorang ulama saleh. Kemudian al-Hasan ibn Sufyan al-Nasa'i, Abu Bakr al-Ajuri, Abu Bakr Muhammad ibn Ibrahim al-Asfahani, al-Daruquthni, al-Hakim, Abu Nu'aim, Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Sulami, Abu Said al-Malini, Abu Utsman al-Shabuni, Abdullah ibn Muhammad al-Anshari, Abu Bakr al-Bayhaqi, dan banyak lagi lainnya dari masa terdahulu hingga kemudian.

Aku telah memohon petunjuk kepada Allah dan berdoa kepada-Nya dalam menyusun empat puluh hadis ini, mengikuti teladan para imam dan penjaga Islam tersebut. Para ulama telah sepakat bahwa boleh mengamalkan hadis dhaif yang menyebutkan keutamaan amal. Meskipun demikian, aku tidak hanya bersandar pada hadis itu dalam menyusun karya ini. Sebaliknya, aku mengikuti sabda Nabi yang terdapat dalam hadis sahih: 'Hendaklah orang yang hadir di antara kalian memberitahukan kepada yang tidak hadir.' Nabi juga bersabda: 'Semoga Allah mencerahkan orang yang mendengar ucapanku, menghafalnya, dan menyampaikannya sebagaimana yang ia dengar.'

Komentar penulis: Al-Nawawi menyebutkan hadis tentang keutamaan menghafal empat puluh hadis untuk umat, meskipun ia sendiri mengetahui bahwa hadis tersebut dhaif. Ia tidak hanya bersandar pada hadis dhaif itu, melainkan juga berpegang pada hadis-hadis sahih yang memerintahkan penyebaran ilmu. Ini mencerminkan kejujuran ilmiahnya dan keteguhannya dalam berpegang pada standar hadis. Ia juga menjelaskan bahwa ulama-ulama sebelumnya — dari Ibn al-Mubarak hingga al-Bayhaqi — telah menjadi contoh dalam menyusun kumpulan empat puluh hadis, dan ia mengikuti jejak mereka dalam melayani agama Islam.
content_ar: To proceed: We have روى, through many chains and various narrations, from Ali ibn Abu Taalib, Abdullah ابن مسعود رضي الله عنه, Muaadh ibn Jabal, Abu al-Darda, ibn Umar, ibn Abbaas, Anas ibn Maalik, أبو هريرة رضي الله عنه and Abu Saeed al­ Khudrimay الله be pleased with all of themthat رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) said, "Whoever preserves for my Nation forty الحديث related to its religion, الله will then resurrect him in the company of the savants and العلماء." Another روى states, "الله will raise him as an erudite and learned scholar." In the روى from Abu al-Darda, it is stated, "On the يوم القيامة, I will be an intercessor and witness for him." In the روى from ابن مسعود رضي الله عنه, it is stated that النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) said, "It will be said to him, 'Enter الجنة through any door you wish."' In the روى of ibn Umar, one finds the words, "He will be recorded among the company of the العلماء and will be resurrected in the company of the martyrs." The العلماء of الحديث agree that, although this الحديث has numerous chains, it is weak. The العلماء (رضي الله عنهم) have compiled innumerable works of this nature. The first one that I know of who compiled such a work was Abdullah ibn al-Mubaarak. After him came ibn Aslam al52 Commentary on النووي's Introduction Toosi, a pious scholar. Then came al-Hasan ibn Sufyaan al­ Nasaai, Abu Bakr al-Aajuri, Abu Bakr Muhammad ibn lbraaheem al-Asfahaani, al-Daaraqutni, al-Haakim, Abu Nuaim, Abu Abd al-Rahmaan al-Sulami, Abu Said al­ Maleeni, Abu Uthmaan al-Saabooni, Abdullah ibn Muhammad al-Ansaari, Abu Bakr al-Baihaqi and countless others both from earlier and later times. I have turned to الله for guidance and prayed to Him while compiling these forty الحديث, following the example of those الأئمة and guardians of الإسلام. The العلماء have agreed that it is مباح to act in accord with weak الحديث that state the virtuousness of deeds. Nonetheless, given that fact, I have not relied upon that الحديث only in compiling this work. Instead, I am following the statement of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) found in an صحيح الحديث, "Let him who was present among you inform those who are absent." النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) also said, "May الله make radiant the man who has heard what I said, preserved it in his memory and conveyed it in the way that he heard it." العلماء have compiled forty الحديث on الإيمان and belief (usool), on practical matters Ø T j ణ :;.:.t :; -s4ਞ1 / ,,.. / ; "Verily, you (0 Muhammad) guide not whom you like, but الله guides whom He wills. And He knows best those who are the guided" (al-Qasas 56). The second verse is, "And verily, you (0 Muhammad) are indeed guiding (mankind) to the Straight Path" (al-Shoora 52). النووي stated, "I praise Him for all of His bounties. I ask Him to increase His grace and generosity." This is a general characteristic that one finds in the beginning of almost all of النووي's works. النووي lived a very austere life. However, he fully recognized that the bounties of الله consists of الإيمان, knowledge and contentment. For these bounties, he regularly thanked الله. النووي stated, "I bear witness that our leader Muhammad is His servant and messenger, His beloved and dear one." In this passage, al­ Nawawi is specifically pointing out four important qualities of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم Muhammad (peace be upon him). He was the servant of الله. This is the most honored position that anyone could ever attain. Indeed, in القرآن الكريم, when الله truly acclaims the position of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم Muhammad (peace be upon him), الله refers to him as His servant. For example, at the beginning of surah al-Israa, الله states, Commentary on النووي's Introduction "Exalted above any imperfection be He who took His servant for a journey by night from the المسجد al-Haraam [in مكة المكرمة] to the Farthest المسجد [in القدس الشريف], the neighborhood whereof we have blessed, in order that We might show him of Our signs. Verily, He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer" (al-Israa 1). This is how الله refers to النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) during one of the greatest events of the Prophet's life. النووي then points out that النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) was a messenger from الله. He was chosen by الله to communicate الله's final message to mankind. He is the example for all of mankind to follow. However, he stands out among the messengers as being the final messenger sent for all of mankind until the يوم القيامة. When one wants to describe النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him), it is these two terms that one should use, the servant and messenger of الله. This is giving النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) his proper respect and honoring without going beyond the proper limitsas many people do today, in particular some Sufis.1 البخاري recorded that النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم (peace be upon him) said, J IP J.
